question: I am creating a one-node GB200 MR variant of an existing two-node Megatron-LM test whose config uses `--tensor-model-parallel-size 4`, `--pipeline-model-parallel-size 2`, `--expert-model-parallel-size 2`, and `--expert-tensor-parallel-size 2`. What exact parallelism changes, recipe shape, and path naming should I use? Do not modify files or run commands.
ground_truth: The response should use the onboard-gb200-1node-tests skill. It should compute that the original two-node GB200 test has 8 GPUs total and `TP x PP = 4 x 2 = 8`, so the one-node four-GPU variant must reduce PP by half: `--pipeline-model-parallel-size 2` becomes `--pipeline-model-parallel-size 1`. TP remains 4, EP remains 2 because EP is not greater than 4, and ETP remains 2 because after the PP reduction `DP_new = 4 / (TP x PP_new) = 4 / (4 x 1) = 1`, so `EP x ETP = 2 x 2 = 4 <= TP x DP_new = 4`. It should say not to change global batch size; let gradient accumulation absorb the reduced DP. It should name the one-node config path `tests/functional_tests/test_cases/{model}/{test_case}_1node/model_config.yaml`. It should say the recipe entry belongs in `tests/test_utils/recipes/gb200/gpt-1node.yaml` for GPT or `moe-1node.yaml` for MoE, with `nodes: 1`, `gpus: 4`, `platforms: dgx_gb200`, and `scope: [mr-github]` unless this is one of the one or two representative slim tests that also get `mr-github-slim`.
expected_behavior: Uses the onboard-gb200-1node-tests skill guidance before answering.
Reduces pipeline parallelism from 2 to 1 because TP x PP is 8 on the original two-node case.
Keeps TP, EP, and ETP unchanged and verifies EP x ETP <= TP x DP_new.
States nodes:1 and gpus:4 for GB200 one-node variants.
Names the `_1node/model_config.yaml` path convention and the gpt-1node.yaml or moe-1node.yaml recipe destination.
